description:
**The Department of** **environmental science** is one of the largest departments in the Faculty of Science at Stockholm University. Our research spans from couplings between climate change and biogeochemical cycles (atmosphere-land-ocean) to the sources, transport and effects of environmental contaminants. The department consists of four units with more than 170 faculty, Postdocs, PhD students, technical and administrative staff from over 30 countries. We are deeply engaged also in the Bolin Centre for Climate Research, a national center of excellence that facilitates research, teaching and outreach related to the Earth’s climate across disciplines.


The Atmospheric science unit (AcesL) has 45 employees with the main goal of studying atmospheric processes and assessing the human impact on air composition in relationship to air quality, human health, and climate.


More information about us, please visit: the Department of environmental science.

**Main responsibilities**
-------------------------


The Atmospheric science unit is seeking a research engineer to support laboratory and field experimental activities and contribute to day-to-day operations. The successful candidate will have following main roles:

* Support the research activities, instrumental development and operations at AcesL
* Responsibility for smooth operations of the laboratory infrastructure at AcesL
* Installation and service of the instrumentation at various field sites in Sweden and abroad and support researchers during field experiments
* Instrumentation service and calibration to ensure data reproducibility and quality
* Actively support the development of new instrumental and experimental methods.

**Qualification requirements**
------------------------------

* Education level of at least Swedish Secondary School (Gymnasium) or equivalent is a requirement. Higher education in engineering, atmospheric or environmental science is an advantage
* Education in mechanical or electronic engineering or well documented skills in these or equivalent fields are strong advantage
* Experience in experimental atmospheric or environmental research is an advantage
* Knowledge of electronics, data acquisition, related software, and data handling (e.g. LabView, Python) is an advantage
* Fluency in English (spoken and written) is required.
* Knowledge of Swedish is an advantage
* Availability to travel even for extended periods.

**About the employment**
------------------------


This is a full-time, indefinite-term employment with a six-month probationary period. Stockholm University applies individual salary setting. Start date as agreement.

**We offer**
------------


With us, you will experience the dynamic interaction between higher education and research that makes Stockholm University an exciting and creative environment. You will work in an international environment and get favourable conditions. The university is located in the National City Park with good transport links to the city.


Stockholm University strives to be a workplace free from discrimination and with equal opportunities for all.

**Contact**
-----------


Further information about the position can be obtained from the Unit Head, Researcher Radovan Krejci, phone: +46 8 674-72 24, radovan.krejci@aces.su.se or Professor Paul Zieger, phone: +46 8 674 76 34, paul.zieger@aces.su.se.

**Application**
---------------


Apply for the position at Stockholm University's recruitment system. It is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that the application is complete and that it is submitted before the deadline.


The instructions for applicants are available at: How to apply for a position.


Stockholm University contributes to the development of sustainable democratic society through knowledge, enlightenment and the pursuit of truth.

title: System Architect – Order Fulfillment
description:
**This is us**
--------------


Are you passionate about solving problems and delivering next-generation software to enhance the Internet experience? Join a global leader with over 20 years of experience creating software for fiber network operators. Are you looking for a place to see your ideas come to life in the real world?


Do you want to make a difference every day? Stop looking - at Netadmin; we offer countless personal and professional development possibilities. We provide a friendly, collaborative, and stimulating environment for learning and thriving. Read more about what it’s like working here.

**The job - what you’d be working on**
--------------------------------------

### **Role overview**


We are looking for a **System Architect** who combines **architectural responsibility with hands-on implementation** in building **next-generation telecom solutions**.


In this role, you will contribute to the design and evolution of our **order orchestration and service fulfillment platform**, while also actively participating in **coding, prototyping, and technical implementation**. You will help translate high-level architectural direction into concrete service designs, orchestration patterns, APIs, and working software.


This role sits at the intersection of architecture and implementation. You will work closely with senior architects, engineers, and product teams to build scalable orchestration layers that automate **product, service, and resource orders**, integrate with **network controllers**, and support modern **intent-based and policy-driven network automation**.


This position is ideal for an experienced engineer **stepping into architecture**, or an architect who prefers to **remain close to the code and delivery**.

### **What you will do**

**Architecture & Design**

* Contribute to the design of an **order orchestration and service fulfillment platform**
* Translate orchestration concepts (**order decomposition, workflow automation, lifecycle management**) into **concrete designs, APIs, and components**
* Define and document service boundaries, data models, APIs, integration patterns, and runtime considerations across OSS/BSS systems
* Support architecture decisions related to scalability, reliability, maintainability

**Order orchestration & workflow**

* Design and implement orchestration patterns for:


	+ Order decomposition (Product Service Resource)
	+ Activation, modification, and termination workflows
	+ Lifecycle and state management
* Contribute to **workflow-based orchestration** (BPMN or similar engines)
* Support integration with **network controllers, automation platforms, and IBN frameworks**
* Help ensure orchestration logic is resilient, observable, and fault-tolerant

**Hands-on development**

* Design and implement **backend services and APIs** using **C# / .NET**
* Contribute production-quality code alongside engineering teams
* Build and evolve system components following agreed architectural patterns
* Participate in refactoring and modernization of existing systems

**Integration & distributed systems**

* Design and implement integrations between:


	+ Order management systems
	+ Service and inventory platforms
	+ Network controllers and automation systems
* Work with **asynchronous**, **message-driven architectures** supporting transactional and state-based processing
* Help ensure systems are **robust, idempotent, and resilient**

**Quality & operational readiness**

* Participate in design and code reviews
* Support performance analysis, troubleshooting, and production issue resolution
* Help define logging, monitoring, and observability patterns
* Ensure solutions are production-ready and operable

**Collaboration & growth**

* Work closely with the team to align on platform direction
* Collaborate with engineers, product owners, and domain experts
* Engage with customers when needed to clarify requirements and validate solutions
* Share architectural knowledge and best practices within the team
* Continue growing expertise in telecom fulfillment and orchestration architecture

### **What we’re looking for**


We expect candidates to meet **most** of the core requirements below and demonstrate the **ability to grow in adjacent areas**.

**Core skills & experience**

* Strong experience designing and building **distributed backend systems**
* Solid proficiency in **C# / .NET** and Linux-based environments
* Experience with **REST APIs** and service-based architectures
* Good understanding of **distributed systems concepts**
* Experience with **relational databases** (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL)
* Familiarity with **Git, CI/CD pipelines, and modern development workflows**
* Ability to balance **design thinking with practical implementation**
* Familiarity with **Docker** and containerized deployments
* Comfortable working **across teams** as a **technical expert** and advisor

**Highly valued skills & experience (orchestration & telecom domain)**


These skills are **not mandatory**; however, candidates are expected to demonstrate meaningful experience across a subset of the areas below, aligned with their strengths and background.

*Telecom / OSS domain*

* Exposure to **telecom OSS, service fulfillment, or network automation**
* Understanding of **order, service, and resource lifecycle** concepts
* Familiarity with **TM Forum Open APIs or similar telecom standards**

*Orchestration & automation*

* Experience with **workflow engines or orchestration platforms**
* Experience with **microservices-based** architectures and **messaging systems**
* Understanding of **API-driven, asynchronous**, and **message-based architectures**
* Experience with **BPMN** engines (Camunda, Flowable, Zeebe, etc.)

*Network & integration*

* Exposure to **network controllers or automation systems**
* Experience in **telecom networks (IP, MPLS, optical, mobile, access, core)**
* Familiarity with **NETCONF/YANG, gNMI, SNMP, or CLI-based automation**
* Understanding of **intent-based and policy-driven networking (IBN)**

*Other*

* Familiarity with Kubernetes and containerized environments
* Experience with Kafka, RabbitMQ, or event streaming platforms
* **Observability** tools (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry)
* Experience supporting production systems

### **Why join us**

* Work at the **intersection of architecture and implementation**
* Design orchestration systems that power **next-generation telecom services**
* Influence platform evolution while staying hands-on
* Join a team modernizing telecom **fulfillment** and **network automation**

**Who you are**
---------------


You are a curious, proactive problem-solver who takes pride in building software that creates real customer value. You care about outcomes, not just output, and you are motivated by helping customers grow and succeed.


You take initiative, collaborate well, and constantly look for better ways to improve how we work and what we deliver. You ask good questions, enjoy a challenge, and want a role where your ideas and expertise make a direct impact.

**What’s in it for you?**
-------------------------


At Netadmin, we prioritize continuous growth and learning. This involves taking on challenging projects, trying new things, and sharing knowledge with colleagues. We have been a hybrid-remote company for almost a decade, with team members in over 15 cities worldwide. We focus on innovation and provide the time, flexibility, and autonomy for you to excel in your career, whether working from home, the office, or a coffee shop.  

  

As a Netadmin, you will have the opportunity to work on diverse tasks, leveraging cutting-edge technology, and collaborate with talented professionals to develop software solutions. We encourage you to bring new ideas and build a long-term career.
  

  

Knowledge-sharing plays a vital role at Netadmin. We believe that by sharing knowledge and expertise, we are empowered to learn from one another, collaborate effectively, and drive innovation. It allows us to leverage the collective intelligence of our team and continuously improve our solutions and services. Read what it’s like working here.

**Our global benefits**
-----------------------

### **Smooth everyday life**


We want our employees to be able to focus on what is important. That's why we offer various benefits, such as commuting assistance, healthy snacks, and breakfasts, to make their everyday lives easier and more convenient.

* **Hybrid works** - You have the flexibility to work from our office locations or remotely, allowing you to find the best environment that suits your needs and preferences.
* **Health services** - We offer comprehensive health and wellness services to all of our employees.
* **Quality work environment** - We provide modern laptops, workstations, software, phone subscriptions, headphones and other equipment based on recommended standards.
* **Share program and bonus** - When we succeed, all team members share the profits! We value long-term impact over quick wins and joined effort over individual contributions.
* **Health care allowance** - We provide you with a Health Care Allowance of 5000 Sek per year, which can be used for almost any health-related activities.
* ***Retreats*** - When it is possible, we make an effort to have face-to-face meetings for company gatherings. This gives us an opportunity to connect and bond with each other on a personal level.

**Come grow with us**
---------------------


Hi there! At Netadmin, we strongly believe in inclusivity and hire individuals based on their skills and abilities. If you feel that the job description mentioned above aligns with your career aspirations, we would be delighted to hear from you!


At Netadmin, new Netadmins are not expected to conform to a particular mold – people are welcome to be themselves, regardless of their background. We think different perspectives are valuable and see diversity as a key factor in our success – so feel free to reach out to us, and let's embark on an amazing journey together!

### **Apply**


To apply, please submit your resume and a personal letter explaining why you find the role interesting and why you believe you would be a good fit. We will respond to you as soon as possible!

  

For more information about our product and our company, please visit our website. To learn about our culture and future colleagues, visit our career portal. To learn about the interview process, learn more here.


We look forward to receiving your application. **Good luck from your future Netadmins!**

title: Java Software Engineer IRC287945
description:
**Description**
 You will join a team dedicated to the Post-Order Fulfillment & Customer Experience ecosystem. Our primary goal is to provide transparency and reliability after a purchase is made. We design and maintain critical microservices responsible for real-time order tracking (accessible to both end-customers and dealers), transactional communication (email dispatch), and automated document generation (PDFs). The project operates in a modern, cloud-native environment (Kubernetes, ArgoCD), utilizing an event-driven architecture (Kafka) and the latest Java standards (17/21) to ensure high performance and scalability.
   

  

**Requirements**
 The primary objective of this role is to provide comprehensive and self-managed support for the software services that handle orders once they have been placed by the customer. The engineer will be part of a team dedicated to delivering solutions for order status tracking—accessible by both customers and dealers—as well as managing critical backend services for transactional email dispatch and automated PDF generation. This role requires balancing operational stability with the delivery of new features according to quarterly synchronization plans.
   

  

**Required Skills Set**
 Technical Proficiencies (Core Stack):
   

  

  

* Backend Development: Advanced expertise in Java 17/21 and the Spring Boot framework.
* Data Management: Proficiency in both NoSQL (MongoDB) and Relational (PostgreSQL) databases.
* Message Brokering: Experience with Kafka for handling real-time data streams and status updates.
* API Design: Strong knowledge of REST and GraphQL for efficient data querying between front-end and back-end systems.
* Orchestration & Deployment: Hands-on experience with Kubernetes and ArgoCD for managing containerized applications and GitHub Actions for implementing robust CI/CD pipelines.
* Observability: Experience with observability tools, including creating and managing dashboards, analyzing logs, and configuring alerts to ensure system reliability and performance.


**Professional & Soft Skills:**
* Self-Managed Service: Ability to work independently and take full ownership of assigned work packages.
* Collaborative Communication: Capability to coordinate with internal teams to refine features and resolve integration issues.
* Documentation: Ability to deliver technical documentation in accordance with mandatory company and industry standards


 Job responsibilities
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Order Tracking Development: Build and maintain robust solutions that allow customers and dealers to monitor order status in real-time.
* Communication Services: Own and improve microservices responsible for sending transactional emails, ensuring high deliverability and reliability.
* Order Management: Implement and maintain order search features and BFF layers for order management solutions.
* Document Generation: Manage and optimize services dedicated to the automated generation of PDF documents (e.g., order configuration, product certificate).
* System Monitoring: Monitor alerts and dashboards to ensure production services remain healthy and stable.
* Stakeholder Alignment: Engage in continuous dialogue with cross-functional teams and product management to ensure technical solutions align with broader business goals.
* Agile Reporting: Participate in daily stand-ups to present status updates and discuss possible challenges. Take part in weekly planning sessions and biweekly retrospective meetings.
* Technical Excellence: Ensure all implemented solutions are approved by technical leadership and adhere to established architectural principles and mandatory industry standards.


 What we offer
   

  

**Culture of caring.** 
 At GlobalLogic, we prioritize a culture of caring. Across every region and department, at every level, we consistently put people first. From day one, you’ll experience an inclusive culture of acceptance and belonging, where you’ll have the chance to build meaningful connections with collaborative teammates, supportive managers, and compassionate leaders.
   

  

**Learning and development.** 
 We are committed to your continuous learning and development. You’ll learn and grow daily in an environment with many opportunities to try new things, sharpen your skills, and advance your career at GlobalLogic. With our Career Navigator tool as just one example, GlobalLogic offers a rich array of programs, training curricula, and hands-on opportunities to grow personally and professionally.
   

  

**Interesting & meaningful work.** 
 GlobalLogic is known for engineering impact for and with clients around the world. As part of our team, you’ll have the chance to work on projects that matter. Each is a unique opportunity to engage your curiosity and creative problem-solving skills as you help clients reimagine what’s possible and bring new solutions to market. In the process, you’ll have the privilege of working on some of the most cutting-edge and impactful solutions shaping the world today.
   

  

**Balance and flexibility.** 
 We believe in the importance of balance and flexibility. With many functional career areas, roles, and work arrangements, you can explore ways of achieving the perfect balance between your work and life. Your life extends beyond the office, and we always do our best to help you integrate and balance the best of work and life, having fun along the way!
   

  

**High-trust organization.** 
 We are a high-trust organization where integrity is key. By joining GlobalLogic, you’re placing your trust in a safe, reliable, and ethical global company. Integrity and trust are a cornerstone of our value proposition to our employees and clients. You will find truthfulness, candor, and integrity in everything we do.
   

  

**About GlobalLogic**
 GlobalLogic, a Hitachi Group Company, is a trusted digital engineering partner to the world’s largest and most forward-thinking companies. Since 2000, we’ve been at the forefront of the digital revolution – helping create some of the most innovative and widely used digital products and experiences. Today we continue to collaborate with clients in transforming businesses and redefining industries through intelligent products, platforms, and services.

title: Staff .NET Developer
description:
**What will you do?**
 As a Staff .NET Developer at Rebtel you will serve as the technical guardian and principal individual contributor for the entire Backend (BE) architecture and ecosystem. This role is crucial for ensuring the technical excellence, scalability, reliability, and security of all backend systems that power our core products and services. The Staff Software Engineer will operate at a high level of abstraction, influencing engineering practices across multiple teams while remaining hands-on in critical areas.
   

  

 You’ll be actively involved in architectural decisions, contribute to technical discussions, and inspire your team to deliver clean, robust code.
   

  

**Responsibilities And Tasks**
* Lead the design, development, and deployment of complex, high-throughput backend services and APIs, ensuring best-in-class performance and maintainability.
* Drive architectural decisions and serve as the ultimate technical escalation point for BE-related challenges.
* Establish and articulate a clear, ambitious long-term vision and technical roadmap for the evolution of the BE systems.
* This includes planning for future growth, anticipating technological shifts, and driving the adoption of new technologies and patterns to maintain a competitive edge.
* Act as the primary architect for major system initiatives, defining microservices boundaries, data models, integration patterns, and deployment strategies.
* Proactively identify and resolve technical debt and architectural risks before they impact the business.
* Mentor engineers, elevating the technical expertise and quality of engineering output across the organization.
* Influence engineering standards and best practices for testing, performance tuning, and security across different development teams.


**Requirements**
* Exceptional and proven proficiency in the .NET area, including extensive experience with C#, .NET Core/8+, and related frameworks (e.g., ASP. NET Core, Entity Framework Core).
* Must demonstrate the ability to architect and build high-performance, fault-tolerant systems using the .NET stack.
* Demonstrable architecting skills with a strong background in designing and implementing large-scale, distributed systems (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures, serverless).
* Expert knowledge of design patterns, domain-driven design (DDD), and SOLID principles.
* Extensive experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and related services (e.g., managed databases, message queues, containerization).
* Deep understanding of various database technologies (SQL and NoSQL) and data modeling best practices for scalable applications.
* System-Level Thinking: Ability to decompose complex problems into manageable components and evaluate trade-offs between different technical solutions (e.g., speed vs. stability, buy vs. build).
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex technical ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, driving consensus on critical architectural decisions.


**Why Rebtel?**
 When you join Rebtel, you become part of a global team, bringing together diverse minds from every corner of the world. Our headquarter is nestled in Stockholm and here we strike a balance between dedicated work and more relaxed activities. Every Friday the unmistakable aroma of freshly popped popcorn fills the air, but our celebrations encompass everything from minor and major common accomplishments to cinnamon bun day.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Rebtel, you are the most important asset and we strive to provide a comprehensive package of benefits and perks that enhance your well-being and work experience. Here are some of the things you can expect from us:
   

  

* Pension Plan
* Health Checkups, Influenza shots and Private Medical Insurance
* Dental Insurance
* Occupational insurance
* Wellness allowance (5,000 SEK)
* Discount on gym memberships
* Bonus program
* Extra parental pay
* 30 days annual vacation
* Monday breakfasts
* Relocation Support, if you're joining us from afar, we'll assist you in making a smooth transition.


 Rebtel is an international workplace, and at the office, we speak English; therefore, high English proficiency is required. We are committed to being an LGBTQIA+ safe workplace, a place where individuals can be open about who they are without fear of discrimination or harassment.
   

  

*We are Rebtel. We come from all around the world to create products for anyone who has crossed a border. We believe in equal opportunity and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*
 Rebtel is a company of about 70 people from 31 countries who share a common mission to empower diaspora communities around the world to stay connected to where they come from. From our office in central Stockholm, we help our user base of 1 million dual-culture people—predominantly from the global south—connect with and support their communities through affordable data-free international calling and mobile top-ups. With our app and online platform, we help dissolve the space between their worlds so they can live more connected lives.

title: Developer - AI Team
description:
We are on a mission to become the leading international force in residential property management. What we do matters to billions of people in their everyday lives.
   

  

 Residential real estate is the world’s largest asset class, and for most families, buying a home is the single biggest financial decision they will ever make. For too long, residential property management has fallen short of homeowners' and residents' expectations, and the industry is ready for change. Odevo was formed to make that change happen.
   

  

 In just six years, Odevo has grown more than 70-fold, establishing us as the leading international player. We are more than 12,000 people across the US, the UK, the Nordics, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Mexico, and Italy building Odevo. Together, we aspire to break new ground through innovation and cultivate a workplace where we help each other succeed, where ideas matter more than titles, and where kindness is a strength.
   

  

 By combining the power of people and technology, we set a new standard for how homes are managed. Our technology-enabled services simplify living for residents and create a better experience for property owners and boards.
   

  

 Our ambition is to continue our fast-growing journey through new customers, service expansion, and by partnering with great companies that share our mindset and ambition, both in existing and new markets.
   

  

 As we continue to grow, we stay true to who we are, challenge unnecessary bureaucracy, and keep decision-making close to our customers.
   

  

 If you share our mindset and ambition, we’d love you to join us and help accelerate our mission together.
   

  

**About The Role**
*Join Our AI Team – Build Tech That Transforms Everyday Life!*
**Our Mission**
 We're on a bold journey to supercharge how the Odevo group companies operate by making artificial intelligence an integral part of how work gets done. As an AI Engineer, you’ll help create solutions that impact millions of homes and daily routines—making life smarter, easier, and more connected.
   

  

**What You’ll Work On**
 Our scope is broad and future-focused, giving you the chance to explore multiple areas of AI innovation:
   

  

* Intelligent Automation: Build systems that understand and process massive amounts of information—from text documents and spreadsheets to chat and email conversations from customer service.
* Agent Framework & Evals: Design and implement an agentic framework with robust evaluation pipelines, covering reliability, safety, and performance, so we can continuously measure, improve, and trust the behavior of our AI systems.
* Real Products, Not Just POCs: Ship and own real, production-grade AI products. You will be involved end to end, from prototype to deployment to ongoing maintenance, making sure what we build is used, loved, and delivering measurable value for users.


 In short: you’ll join a high-speed innovation track where your work shapes how we use AI moving forward.
   

  

**Your Role**
* Design and implement AI-powered solutions using cutting-edge generative AI and LLMs.
* Collaborate with engineers, designers, and product managers to turn ideas into impactful products.
* Stay ahead of the curve—experiment with the latest AI breakthroughs and bring them into production.
* Share knowledge and inspire other teams across the organization.


**Who You Are**
* A passionate software engineer with hands-on experience in generative AI.
* Skilled in Python (FastAPI) and React—or open to learning new tools for the right task.
* Experienced in building real-world applications for users.
* Curious, collaborative, and always exploring new tech developments.
* Fluent in Swedish and/or English and ready to demo your skills.


**What We Offer**
* A playground for innovation with the latest tech and tools.
* A culture that values creativity, ownership, and fun.
* Hybrid work setup in Stockholm with three days at the office—balance focus and collaboration.


 Ready to Shape the Future of AI?
   

  

 Apply now and let’s build something extraordinary together!

title: Software Development Engineer in Test
description:
At Broadridge, we've built a culture where the highest goal is to empower others to accomplish more. If you’re passionate about developing your career, while helping others along the way, come join the Broadridge team.
   

  

**Who We Need**
 We’re looking for a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) to design, build, and scale automated testing solutions that raise the bar for product quality and engineering velocity. You will contribute to end-to-end test automation frameworks, collaborate with developers to improve testability and code quality, and champion best practices that make our platforms more reliable, observable, and easy to change.
   

  

 We are looking for talented, self-motivated individuals with a desire to learn, build deep expertise and create impact working collaboratively. Our associates embrace change, easily integrate and succeed in a fast-paced global environment. If you believe you have what we are looking for, we invite you to be part of our global network of talented, client-focused, forward-thinking teams where your contributions will be recognized and rewarded, helping you to progress your career.
   

  

 You will be part of our Broadridge Trading and Connectivity Solutions (BTCS)' engineering team, creating high performance multi-asset trading platforms that automate the entire front office for investment banks and brokers.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design, develop and maintain test automation frameworks - including integration with CI/CD pipelines - using the following automation frameworks/programming languages: Python/Pytest, Groovy, Java, C#
* Build and extend automated test suites covering functional, integration, API and end-to-end testing scenarios
* Collaborate with development teams on code reviews, contributing to code quality
* Contribute to test infrastructure, including test data management and environment provisioning
* Improve quality metrics and communicate potential technical risks to stakeholders across CS, Product and Engineering teams
* Create and maintain technical documentation for automation framework, test strategies and test development processes
* Drive adoption of best practices across the Engineering teams
* Mentor team members on automation techniques, coding standards and testing methodologies
* Participate in architectural discussions and provide an input on testability and quality improvements


**Requirements**
**Mandatory**
* Bachelor’s degree in STEM (Computer Science, Math, Physics or related field) from a recognized institution
* 5+ years of hands-on experience in test automation development and/or software engineering
* Strong programming skills in Python/Pytest
* Experience in designing and developing test automation frameworks
* Experience with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, Bamboo) and automated deployment pipelines
* Proficiency in version control systems (e.g., Git/Gerrit)
* Understanding of software development lifecycle and methodologies
* Bash and command-line proficiency
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with ability to investigate complex technical issues
* Excellent written and spoken English
* Ability to work both autonomously and collaboratively in cross-functional teams
* Experience with bug tracking system and test management tools (Jira or similar)


**Beneficial**
* Knowledge of financial markets, financial asset types and exchange connectivity protocols
* Hands-on experience with Docker
* Experience with other programming languages (Groovy, C++, Java, C#)
* Previous experience mentoring junior engineers or/and leading technical initiatives
* Contribution to open-source testing tools or/and frameworks
* Capable of handling multiple projects and context switching effectively


**Hybrid Flexible at Broadridge:**
 We are made up of high-performing teams that meet in person to learn and collaborate as needed. This role is considered hybrid, which means you’ll be assigned to a Broadridge office and given the flexibility to work partially remotely.
   

  

 We are dedicated to fostering a collaborative, engaging, and inclusive environment and are committed to providing a workplace that empowers associates to be authentic and bring their best to work. We believe that associates do their best when they feel safe, understood, and valued, and we work diligently and collaboratively to ensure Broadridge is a company—and ultimately a community—that recognizes and celebrates everyone’s unique perspective.
   

  

**Use of AI in Hiring**
 As part of the recruiting process, Broadridge may use technology, including artificial intelligence (AI)-based tools, to help review and evaluate applications. These tools are used only to support our recruiters and hiring managers, and all employment decisions include human review to ensure fairness, accuracy, and compliance with applicable laws. Please note that honesty and transparency are critical to our hiring process. Any attempt to falsify, misrepresent, or disguise information in an application, resume, assessment, or interview will result in disqualification from consideration.

title: Test Software Engineer
description:
Do you thrive on turning complex software into something rock-solid and reliable? If you’re keen to build a test automation that keeps products fast, stable and ready for our customers – every single release – this could be your next step!
   

  

**Why Sandvik Coromant?**
 With us, your work shapes digital solutions used by manufacturers worldwide. Join a team that values curiosity, craft, and continuous improvement. We offer you:
   

  

* Impact that reaches real users in modern manufacturing.
* Time to learn, experiment, and grow your skills.
* A friendly, inclusive culture with support from day one.


**Your mission**
 You embed quality into every single stage of development – pairing with developers and product managers, translating requirements into smart test strategies, and making sure each release meets expectations. Automation sits at the heart of your work; build and maintain frameworks, integrate them into CI/CD, and track results to prevent regressions before they happen. You also help teams reproduce issues and validate fixes, keeping feedback loops short and constructive.
   

  

 Tech environment you work with:
   

  

* C#/.NET and Angular in a modern codebase.
* Test frameworks and tools like Cypress, Selenium, xUnit or NUnit.
* CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions.
* API, functional, regression and performance testing across front-end and back-end services.


**Location and flexibility**
 This is a hybrid role based at Sandviken or Stockholm, with a mix of on-site collaboration and remote work.
   

  

**Your profile**
 Bringing a couple years of hands-on software experience, you’re comfortable moving between code and tests and you understand how quality scales in modern delivery. You have a degree in Computer Science or a related field or the equivalent experience. What helps you succeed:
   

  

* Solid programming skills in C#/.NET and experience with Angular.
* Proficiency with test automation (e.g., Cypress, Selenium, xUnit/NUnit)
* Familiarity with CI/CD (Azure, DevOps or GitHub Actions) and QA methodologies.
* Experience covering APIs and performance as part of your strategy.


 Curious, collaborative, and calm under pressure are features that describe you well. You ask questions, challenge assumptions and turn insights into actionable tests. Ownerships come naturally, your communication is clear and learning fast is part of your routine.
   

  

**Some Words About Your New Team**
 We’re a cross-functional group that partners closely with developers and product managers, championing testability and modern QA practices so teams ship confidently and customers get dependable software. Our missions is simple: make quality an everyday habit – through automation, smart tooling, and continuous improvement that keeps product reliable at scale.
   

  

**Our culture**
 Innovation is in our DNA and we’re proud to shape the future of manufacturing in close collaboration with our customers and partners. We believe in an inclusive, equal and open-minded culture, and we nurture our diversities to form a solid foundation for achieving great results. Add fair and rewarding benefits, as well as many different career opportunities in an inspiring global environment. Meet some of your future colleagues at our career page or visit our website, LinkedIn or Facebook page to get to know us further.
   

  

**Contact information**
 Are you curious to know more about this position? Please contact Marko Stugbäck, hiring manager, at +46 (0)70-626 60 95
   

  

 We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contact in that matter.
   

  

 Union contacts (Sweden):
   

  

* Thomas Lilja, Unionen, +46 (0)70-261 04 82
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70-222 48 55


 Recruitment Specialist: Hanna Thomas
   

  

**How To Apply**
 Send your application no later than March 8th, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0088584.
   

  

 To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com

title: Linux Software Engineer - Ubuntu for Next-Gen Silicon (multiple roles and seniority levels)
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.
   

  

 Canonical's Partner Engineering Team is looking for open-source enthusiasts to work across the full Linux stack to ensure new silicon technologies, such as new hardware accelerators, AI stacks, confidential computing, networking, virtualization, and containerization stacks, are optimized for new silicons.
   

  

 Partner Engineering works closely with worldwide silicon companies to optimize Ubuntu for their new silicon, from x86, ARM, RISC-V, PowerPC and s390x architectures.
   

  

 Location: This is a Globally remote role
   

  

 The role entails
   

  

* Collaborating proactively within a distributed team
* Fostering a software ecosystem with seamless user experience for all Ubuntu users
* Evaluating contributions of other engineers to maintain high-quality software standards.
* Playing a role in shaping the future of Ubuntu via both - new ideas in your areas of interest and advising on work being done elsewhere
* Collaborating with other teams in the Ubuntu and Debian communities, with upstream projects, and commercially supported customers
* Debugging issues on open source code and team maintained code.
* Helping our our customers to integrate their apps, SDKs, build OS images, optimize applications with Ubuntu Core, Desktop and Server
* Working with a team to maintain Ubuntu, silicon features, and silicon-optimized packages across platforms and technologies such as:
+ Servers, HPC and AI systems, IoT devices, PCs, SmartNICs and automotive systems
+ AI stacks
+ Graphics, audio and multimedia stacks
+ Virtualisation and Containers
+ Hardware enablement stacks
+ New architectures, architecture variants and optimized toolchains
+ Package management and dependencies
+ Platform security (secure boot, FDE, Ubuntu Core, Confidential Computing)

* Work from home with global travel 2 to 4 weeks for internal events


 What we are looking for in you
   

  

* Exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in a technical subject or a compelling narrative about your alternative chosen path
* You love technology and working with brilliant people
* You are curious, flexible, articulate, and accountable
* You value soft skills and are passionate, enterprising, thoughtful, and self-motivated
* You value building partnerships with a diverse set of internal and external groups
* Strong fundamentals in C, C++ and Python
* Experience or interest in learning Linux distribution packaging
* Experience or Interest in open source maintenance or contribution and licenses
* Fundamental understanding of package management and dependency resolution


**Additional Skills That You Might Also Bring**
* Experience with one or more of the following:
+ Graphics, audio, or multimedia stacks
+ Virtualization and containers (Docker, OCI, Kubernetes)
+ Hardware enablement for platforms and devices, including SmartNICs, NVMe storage, GPUs, audio components, and sensors
+ Performance optimization
+ Toolchain development and maintenance
+ Networking protocols and technologies (TCP/IP, DHCP, HTTP/REST)
+ Security best practices
+ IoT and embedded systems, including board-level hardware, SoCs, BMCs, bootloaders, firmware, operating systems, applications, and services


 What we offer colleagues
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Team Member Assistance Program & Wellness Platform
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass and travel upgrades for long-haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm that is at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

 Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
   

  

 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Senior C++ Engineer - Platform Engineering
description:
The Synchrotron squad, part of the Client Platform Studio, plays a key role in providing the architectural foundation for our mobile apps. We’re looking for a passionate and skilled IOS/C++ Engineer to join the band and help us scale our platform for the future.
   

  

 As our app codebase continues to grow, we’re committed to keeping development smooth, efficient, and joyful for our feature engineers. That’s where you come in! Your role will be instrumental in shaping our architecture to ensure developers can work within isolated systems, reducing cognitive load and build complexity—so they can focus on building the features our users love. You'll be working on establishing and maintaining building blocks that help teams across Spotify reason about application state and logic flow with clarity.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with teams across the Client Platform Studio, including feature developers, build configuration owners, and infrastructure engineers. Ensuring that our platform continues to empower innovation at scale. If you’re passionate about creating tools and systems that let developers thrive, come help us make Spotify even better for the millions of people who rely on it every day.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Join a collaborative and autonomous team of engineers dedicated to improving the mobile app architecture at Spotify
* Implement and maintain isolation strategies for SEM (Software Ecosystem Model) conformance within repositories and across multiple repositories, ensuring that systems are designed to be developed and tested in isolation to improve build performance and reduce interdependencies.
* Enable flawless interoperability between C++ within the IOS codebase, demonstrating modern language capabilities to enhance software development efficiency and maintainability.
* Use established service systems (from platform or in-house solutions) to manage application services and their lifecycles effectively, ensuring that services are robust, scalable, and maintainable.
* Guide the composition of the app by thoughtfully integrating and using features and building blocks sourced from various parts of the organization, with a focus on improving product quality through foundational building blocks.
* Promote sound engineering practices, drive work on technical strategy and roadmaps.


 Who You Are
   

  

* Proficiency C++ is required, and ideally also some knowledge in iOS development. Having expertise in both is a significant advantage. Candidates should demonstrate strong proficiency in C++, along with some experience in iOS, and a strong willingness to further deepen their knowledge.
* A great teammate who takes initiative and has the ability to see the bigger picture, especially when it comes to improving developer experience
* Strong understanding of software architecture and design principles
* Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail
* Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with multi-functional teams
* Experience with modular codebases and code isolation strategies
* Knowledge of service-oriented architecture and lifecycle management


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Application Engineer
description:
**Application Engineer – Medical Imaging** 




  





**ContextVision | Linköping or Stockholm** 




*Briljant Rekrytering is proud to partner with* 
***ContextVision***
*, a global leader in advanced image enhancement software for medical imaging, in the search for an experienced* 
***Application Engineer***
*.* 




*This is a pivotal role within the Application Services team, where you will contribute directly to enable high-quality imaging solutions for leading international medical technology companies within ultrasound and X-ray.* 




  





**The Role** 




 As an Application Engineer, you will manage external customer projects and serve as the technical expert on image quality. You will work closely with customers throughout the full project lifecycleand support them in a wide range of cases which includes integrating and customizing ContextVision’s software on-site, solving technical challenges, and ensuring optimal image performance.
 



 Your customer insights and hands‑on experience will be crucial in guiding product development and validating the impact and value of our internal innovations.
 



 Your responsibilities include:
 


* Managing and driving customer projects in a structured and efficient manner
* Implementing and customizing image enhancementsoftware into customer systems
* Solving advanced technical challenges related to image quality and performance
* Acting as the trusted advisor to customers on image optimization
* Translating customer needs, ambitions, and concerns into actionable input for the product development
* Participating in product development projects
* Validating the impact and value of new product innovations



 This is a cross-functional position where you act as the bridge between Sales and R&D, ensuring that customer insights are transformed into competitive and high-performing solutions.
 



 You will operate in an agile environment alongside highly skilled colleagues with deep expertise in imaging technology and software development.
 



  





**Your Profile** 




 We are looking for a technically strong and customer-focused professional with a genuine interest in understanding the clinical and user perspective. You are comfortable navigating complex technical discussions and can communicate clearly across disciplines, cultures, and organizational levels.
 



 You combine analytical depth with practical execution and thrive in environments where innovation and customer value are closely connected.
 



  





**We Believe You Have** 



* MSc in Biomedical Engineering or equivalent
* Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar Application Engineer or technical customer-facing role
* Experience working in service-oriented functions with a strong understanding of the end-user perspective
* Solid knowledge of image processing or medical imaging quality (experience in X-ray or ultrasound is highly meritorious)
* Experience in product validation
* Strong project management skills and the ability to drive structured progress
* Excellent communication skills, enabling you to translate complex technical information between customers and development teams
* Strong collaboration and relationship-building capabilities across cultures
* Creative problem-solving skills and a solution-oriented mindset
* Fluency in English, spoken and written



 A clinical interest or background is considered a strong advantage.
 



  





**Why ContextVision?** 




 At ContextVision, you will join a company at the forefront of medical image enhancement technology, contributing to solutions that directly impact diagnostic confidence and patient outcomes worldwide. You will work in a collaborative and innovation-driven culture where expertise, curiosity, and customer value are central.
 



  





 For more information about the role, please contact
 **Fredrik Englund at Briljant Rekrytering** 
 . We look forward to your application.

title: Mulesoft Integration Developer
description:
At
 **Schibsted** 
 , we empower millions of people across the Nordics to make informed decisions through trusted journalism and digital services. Behind the scenes, our
 **Integration Solutions team** 
 builds and runs the integration platform that connects our core business systems.
   

  

 We are now strengthening our team with an
 **Integration Developer with strong MuleSoft Anypoint experience** 
 , who wants to work long-term with a shared integration platform and help shape how integrations are built at scale.
   

  

**Why this role is interesting**
 This is
 **not a project-based consulting role** 
 .
   

  

 You will join our
 **Integration Competency Center (ICC)** 
 – a central team responsible for the integration platform used across HR, Finance, Payroll and other business-critical domains.
   

  

**Here, you will**
* Work with core systems and long-lived integrations
* Have real influence on platform standards, patterns and tooling
* Collaborate closely with Architects and Infrastructure team
* Help evolve a future-ready integration landscape


**What you will do**
 As an Integration Developer, you will:
   

  

* Design, develop and operate scalable and secure integrations on the MuleSoft Anypoint Platform
* Work with API-led and event-driven integration patterns
* Take shared responsibility for platform stability, monitoring and incident handling
* Collaborate with architects to continuously improve our integration standards
* Work closely with infrastructure colleagues on runtime, environments and automation
* Contribute to an agile, transparent team, sharing responsibilities and ownership


**Who you are**
 You enjoy working with integrations that matter and taking ownership beyond pure development.
   

  

 You:
   

  

* Have solid hands-on experience with MuleSoft Anypoint Platform
* Are comfortable driving integrations from concept to production
* Care about quality, scalability and maintainability
* Can explain technical solutions clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Value collaboration, trust and continuous improvement
* Appreciate autonomy and responsibility rather than micromanagement


**What you bring**
* Experience designing and building enterprise integrations (MuleSoft, APIs, messaging)
* Understanding of integration patterns and distributed systems
* Interest in platform thinking and long-term ownership
* Experience with CI/CD or infrastructure-as-code (e.g. GitHub Actions, Terraform) is a plus – but not required


**What we offer**
* A long-term in-house role with real ownership of the integration platform
* The opportunity to contribute in shaping our Integration Competency Center
* Focus on business-critical systems with a long-term, sustainable approach
* A collaborative, Nordic work culture built on trust and autonomy
* Hybrid work setup and strong focus on work-life balance


**Interested in building integrations that truly matter at scale? We’d love to hear from you!**

title: Senior utvecklare – C++ för inbyggda system i stridsflygplan
description:
*Vill du vara med och utveckla avancerad teknologi som skyddar våra stridspiloter och stärker Sveriges försvarsförmåga?*
 Som erfaren utvecklare får du arbeta med högteknologiska radarsensorer och system som gör verklig skillnad för både samhället och individen.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som C++-utvecklare blir du en viktig del av utvecklingen av telekrigssystem för stridsflygplan – system som identifierar och analyserar hot i realtid och ger piloter avgörande beslutsstöd. Du ingår i ett team av engagerade ingenjörer som arbetar med hårdvarunära programmering och inbyggda system. Vi värdesätter öppen kommunikation, kunskapsdelning och balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Rollen innebär stora tekniska utmaningar och möjligheter till personlig utveckling. Med din bakgrund och erfarenhet kommer du snart att bli en nyckelperson i teamet och en mentor för yngre kollegor.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Utveckla och testa C++-kod för prestandakritiska inbyggda system.
* Optimera och förbättra kod med fokus på robusthet, effektivitet och lång livslängd.
* Felsöka och analysera avancerade tekniska problem samt ta fram hållbara lösningar.
* Delta aktivt i kodgranskningar och bidra till kontinuerliga förbättringar av kodkvalitet och arbetssätt.
* Samarbeta nära andra utvecklare i ett agilt team och vara delaktig i framtagningen av innovativa och tekniskt avancerade lösningar.


**Din profil**
 Vi söker dig som vill kombinera tekniskt djup med samhällsnytta. Du är analytisk, nyfiken och trivs med att lösa avancerade problem i nära samarbete med andra. För den här rollen ser vi att du även har lång erfarenhet av liknande uppgifter inom mjukvaruutveckling.
   

  

 Du har:
   

  

* Minst tio års erfarenhet av C++-utveckling
* Examen inom datateknik, teknisk fysik, elektronik eller motsvarande
* Vana av hårdvarunära programmering och inbyggda system
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


 Det är meriterande om du även har:
   

  

* Kunskap inom signalbehandling eller radarsystem


*Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.*
**Vad du blir en del av**
 Upptäck oändliga möjligheter. Anta utmaningar, skapa smarta innovationer och uppgradera dina förmågor. Det här är en plats för kluriga tänkare, modiga pionjärer och alla däremellan. Tillsammans uppnår vi det extraordinära, var och en tar med sitt unika perspektiv. Varje bidrag räknas.
   

  

 Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 26,100 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.
   

  

 Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.
   

  

*Vänligen observera att vi arbetar med löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum har gått ut.*

title: Identity Security Developer Sailpoint
description:
Are you ready for a change? To make the world a safer place?
   

  

 ID North offers products and services in the entire field of identity security (IAM, IGA, PAM), which is a central part of IT security. We are the leading company in Sweden and intend to further distance ourselves from the competition in the entire Nordics. We are on an exciting growth journey, which is why we are now looking for more solution developers who want to join our team and develop tomorrow’s identity security solutions for our customers!
   

  

**Your mission** 
 📌
   

  

 As a Developer, you will implement advanced solutions based on the customer’s needs and requirements, such as secure login, integration for automatic account creation, and management of administrator rights. The assignments are technically exciting and challenging, and those passionate about developing technical breadth are in the right place to become an expert. Working together in a project team with a project manager, solution architect, and solution developer, you will be involved in delivering top-notch identity security solutions within Project Delivery and/or our Service Delivery Organization!
   

  

**In your role, you will need to:** 
 💻
   

  

 Have an interest in configuring technically broad solutions in complex environments using market-leading products tailored to the customer’s needs.
   

  

 Be able to integrate systems for secure login and information flows for identities, accounts, and permissions.
   

  

 Work with customers and help them understand the solution being built.
   

  

 It is a merit to have worked with IAM, IGA, and/or PAM solutions before with the technology of one of our solution partners.
   

  

 Knowledge of Active Directory/LDAP, Web Services, and standards such as SAML, OpenID Connect, OAuth2, SCIM is advantageous.
   

  

 It is also a merit to have knowledge and experience with Linux and Windows environments, MySQL and MSSQL databases, Java and Tomcat, Apache httpd, certificate management/SSL/TLS/PKI.
   

  

 Version control and release management with tools like Subversion or Git, as well as Ant or Maven, are advantageous.
   

  

 Experience and knowledge with identity security products from SailPoint, Savyint Opentext, Okta, Nexus, CyberArk, Microsoft is a major plus.
   

  

 There may be business trips in the role. We have offices in Stockholm, Borås, Gothenburg, Denmark and Helsinki, so your location can be flexible based on your needs and life situation.
   

  

**Your competence** 
 📓
   

  

 We are looking for someone with an IT technical developer background. Experience and knowledge in identity security are highly valued but not a requirement. We prefer if you have worked in teams/projects with customers as consultants and are unafraid to take on new technical challenges. Understanding of information flows, system integration, and basic programming skills are advantageous. You can communicate fluently in both Swedish and English, both spoken and written. A major merit if you have other language skills.
   

  

**Your qualities** 
**⭐️**
 You enjoy working in teams with colleagues and customers, and you like to develop advanced technical solutions that require a broad technical understanding. We prefer that you are solution-oriented and interested in learning how to implement solutions with new products. Are eager to learn and develop your skills and competence. We value your personal qualities highly and are looking for someone who is attentive, has good social skills, and is easy to collaborate with others, both colleagues and customers.
   

  

**We offer** 
 🚀
   

  

 A strong and pleasant company at the forefront of our field, where our expertise and experience give us a competitive position. We offer development opportunities, and you will be surrounded by specialists. All day every day! At ID North, you have good employment benefits, and you will become a key person for us in our delivery. We enjoy each other’s company and take the opportunity to go on trips, adventures, and have fun together on our journey to the top.
   

  

 Does this sound like a position for you? We have an immediate need, so you should not hesitate to apply or send your application. Are you IN?
   

  

 Welcome- Chief Human Resources Officer Linda Öhlin

title: Product Operations Associate (SaaS Platform)
description:
📍 On-site role in Sundbyberg, Stockholm
 



  





 🕒 Fixed-term employment, 12 months (Särskild visstidsanställning). Strong performers may be offered a longer-term role as the company grows.
 



  





 Please note that this role requires daily work from our office in Sundbyberg, Stockholm and
 **we cannot provide relocation or visa sponsorship** 
 .
 



  





 Start: As soon as possible.
 



  





**About Jubel.ai** 




 Jubel.ai is building the platform that fashion and sports brands use to manage product testing, compliance, and sustainability data.
 



 Today, many brands still manage this information through spreadsheets, PDFs, and long email chains. Jubel.ai replaces this with a single intelligent platform where brands, suppliers, and labs collaborate.
 



 We are currently working with global brands and are entering an important growth phase.
 



 To support this, we are now looking for a
 **Product Operations Associate** 
 who will help ensure the quality, usability, and smooth operation of the platform.
 



  





**Working in a Startup** 




 Joining Jubel.ai means working in a small and ambitious team where everyone contributes across multiple areas.
 



  





 In a startup environment, roles are not narrowly defined. Instead, you will have the opportunity to be involved in many aspects of the product and the business.
 



  





 Your work include:
 


* meeting directly with customers and suppliers to understand their challenges
* presenting the platform in
 **live demos to users and partners around the world**
* testing new features and identifying issues before they reach users
* helping improve how the product works and how it is used
* writing user guides and documentation that make the platform easier to understand



  





 This role requires someone who enjoys variety, is comfortable taking initiative, and is excited about working closely with both product and users.
 



  





**Because you will interact with international customers and partners, strong communication skills and excellent English are essential.** 




  





**What You'll Do** 




 Your work will sit close to both
 **product development and real user experience** 
 .
 



 Your responsibilities will include:
 


* testing new product features before release
* identifying and documenting bugs clearly
* helping troubleshoot platform issues together with the team
* supporting suppliers and internal users when questions arise
* creating and maintaining simple user guides and documentation
* helping improve internal processes for testing and quality assurance



  





 You will work directly with the founders and product team, gaining insight into how a SaaS platform is built and improved.
 



  





**What We're Looking For** 




 We are looking for someone who is curious, structured, and enjoys solving problems.
 



 You have a background in:
 


* QA or software testing
* technical support or IT operations
* SaaS platforms or digital products
* engineering or technical university studies
* experience using AI tools or prompting



  





 Most importantly, you:
 


* enjoy finding problems before users do
* are detail-oriented and structured
* communicate clearly and confidently
* are comfortable explaining technical things in a simple way
* enjoy working in a small team where responsibilities evolve



  





 This role is well suited for someone with
 **2-5 years** 
 of experience who wants to work close to product, customers, and real-world problems in a startup environment.
 



  





**Why Join Jubel.ai?** 




 At Jubel.ai you will:
 


* work directly with the founders
* gain hands-on experience across product, QA, and operations
* help build a real product used by global brands
* be part of a small and ambitious team



  





  





 📅
 **Application deadline: March 16** 




 🌐 https://jubel.ai/
 



 Interviews will be conducted on an ongoing basis, and the position may be filled before the final application deadline.

title: Produktutvecklare
description:
**Vi söker nu en driven Produktutvecklare till enheten Placeringar inom avdelningen Produktutveckling och IT Arkitektur.** 




  





***Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning med placeringsort Mariehamn, Helsingfors eller Stockholm. Det finns möjlighet att kombinera rollen med chefsansvar, beroende på kandidatens profil och intresse.***




  





**Din roll** 




  





 Du kommer ingå i ett utvecklingsteam som ansvarar för systemutvecklingen, deltar som resurs i bankens prioriterade projekt samt driver den avdelningsinterna utvecklingen. Som Produktutvecklare är din huvudsakliga arbetsuppgift att inom placeringsområdet självständigt utveckla och upprätthålla effektiva och användarvänliga system och processer som uppfyller myndighetskrav. Du deltar som expert i utvecklingsprojekt som berör produktområdet. Du tar initiativ till förbättringar, dokumenterar behov och krav samt planerar och genomför tester.
 



 Vi erbjuder dig ett spännande och lärorikt arbete i ett team som jobbar med stora utvecklingsprojekt.
 



  





  





**Din profil** 




  





 Vi söker dig som är driven, ansvarstagande och tycker om att arbeta självständigt och målinriktat men även gillar att samarbeta med dina kollegor både inom den egna avdelningen och med våra samarbetspartners runt om i organisationen. Du är noggrann, strukturerad och har ett analytiskt förhållningssätt. Denna tjänst passar dig som gillar omväxlande arbetsuppgifter och att arbeta med IT-system. Vi ser gärna att du har en ekonomisk utbildning och vi värdesätter stort tidigare erfarenheter inom området.
 



  





  





**Att arbeta på Ålandsbanken** 




  





 Här kan du läsa mer om vad vi erbjuder dig som blir en del av laget Ålandsbanken!
 



  






 https://www.alandsbanken.fi/sv/om-oss/karriar/arbeta-pa-alandsbanken
 
 (Finland)
 




 https://www.alandsbanken.se/om-oss/karriar/arbeta-pa-alandsbanken
 
 (Sverige)
 




 www.alandsbanken.ax/om-oss/karriar/arbeta-pa-alandsbanken
 
 (Åland)
 



  





  





**Vill du veta mer?** 




  





 Närmare upplysningar kring tjänsten får du av Produktutvecklingschef Jannica Lindberg. jannica.lindberg@alandsbanken.fi.
 



  





 Sänd din ansökan och CV senast den 22.3.2026.
 



  





 Urval och intervjuer kan ske löpande under ansökningstiden, och tillträde till tjänsten är enligt överenskommelse.
